title: Best Driveway Cleaner of 2026: Find the Right Formula for Your Stains
excerpt: Oil, grease, mold, or mildew? Using the wrong driveway cleaner wastes effort. Match the formula to your stain for spotless concrete, asphalt, or pavers.
author: Editorial Team
metaDescription: Oil, grease, mold, or mildew? Using the wrong driveway cleaner wastes effort. Match the formula to your stain for spotless concrete, asphalt, or pavers.
intro:
Many people grab a single bottle labeled 'driveway cleaner' and expect it to blast through everything—oil spills, green mildew, tire marks. But two weeks later, the stain is still there, and the bottle ends up in the trash.

The real issue isn't the product quality; it's chemistry. Oil and grease cling to concrete pores with a hydrocarbon grip that only poultice powders or strong degreasers can break. Mold, algae, and moss are living organisms that need oxidation or biocides to kill and release from the surface. No single chemical does both well.

This guide sorts the products that actually deliver on their promise by stain type, so you can match the cleaner to the mess before you buy. Choosing the wrong formula leads to hours of scrubbing with little result. Choosing the right one turns a weekend chore into a quick pour, spray, or sprinkle that restores your driveway's look.

products.title: CHOMP! Concrete Oil Stain Remover: Pull It Out Removes and Cleans Oils, Greases from Garage Floors & Driveways 32 Fl Oz
products.label: CHOMP! Pull It Out Oil Remover
products.oneLiner: Liquid poultice that dries and lifts deep oil stains for no-scrub, sweep-away cleanup.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Once the poultice dries to a white powder, sweeping with a stiff-bristle broom or using a shop vac clears the residue and reveals the stain-free concrete beneath.
pros: Removes old, set-in oil stains and fresh spills from porous concrete and asphalt. — 2515 mentions, 76.4% positive
Pour-on application requires zero scrubbing or pressure washing — simply let it dry and sweep away. — 437 mentions, 97% positive
Works across a range of petroleum-based fluids including motor oil, transmission fluid, and brake fluid. — 1553 mentions, 74.3% positive
Bleach-free, acid-free poultice — safer for concrete, brick, and adjacent grass than solvent-based degreasers.
No rinsing needed, reducing water usage and runoff.
cons: Dried poultice can leave a white chalky film on concrete that must be swept or rinsed away after treatment. — 922 mentions of stain removal, 54% negative — many users noted white leftovers on concrete
Drying time may stretch to 48 hours or more in humid weather, delaying use of the cleaned area. — 185 mentions drying time, 42% negative — some report days to fully dry
products.verdict: For driveway oil stains, CHOMP! replaces the effort of scrubbing with a simple pour-and-sweep process; the drying wait and chalky sweep-up are small prices for its deep-cleaning results.
products.review:
CHOMP! Pull It Out tackles oil stains by converting from a liquid to a powder that pulls petroleum up from deep within concrete pores — no scrubbing, no pressure washer. Apply the milky liquid directly to the stain, and the poultice works its way down to lift oil molecules as it dries.

The poultice works on both fresh spills and years-old stains, lifting motor oil, transmission fluid, brake fluid, and other petroleum-based grime. Even heavily soiled garage floors show a marked lightening after a single application, though deep, layered stains may benefit from a second round.

After pouring, the liquid dries to a chalky white film that encapsulates the oil. Sweeping with a stiff broom or using a shop vac reveals the cleaned surface. In humid conditions, drying can extend to a couple of days, and the treated spot must stay dry during that time. The white residue is non-staining and cleans up without harsh chemicals, though it adds a manual step to the process.

Homeowners with oil spots on garage floors, asphalt, or unsealed concrete driveways who want a pour-on, no-scrub solution. Expect to let the product sit undisturbed for at least a few hours — longer in high humidity — and then sweep away the dried powder. This poultice targets petroleum stains exclusively; it is not formulated for mold, mildew, algae, or general surface dirt. Those using pressure washers for organic growth should look at bleach- or peroxide-based cleaners instead.

💡 💡 Tip: Once the poultice dries to a white powder, sweeping with a stiff-bristle broom or using a shop vac clears the residue and reveals the stain-free concrete beneath.

products.title: CAF Outdoor Cleaning EXIMO Waterless Concrete Cleaner, Oil Stain Remover for Asphalt, Driveway, Garage Floor, Basement and Walkway Surfaces, Natural Concrete Cleaner Powder Treatment, 3 lb Container
products.label: CAF Outdoor Cleaning EXIMO Waterless Concrete Cleaner
products.oneLiner: Dry powder that absorbs and degrades oil without water, ideal for no-rinse garages.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Apply on a calm day or shield the area with a cardboard barrier to minimize powder drift during sprinkling.
pros: Effectively lifts old and fresh oil stains from concrete over repeated treatments — 236 mentions, 79.7% positive
Sprinkle-on application requires no scrubbing, hose, or pressure washer — 31 mentions, 97% positive
Waterless process leaves no runoff, making it safe for areas without drainage
cons: Fine powder can drift in windy conditions, creating a dusty mess during application — 16 mentions messiness, 69% negative
Deep oil stains may need multiple applications over several weeks to fully disappear — 152 mentions of stain removal, 35% negative
products.verdict: For waterless oil stain removal that demands nothing more than sprinkling and waiting, EXIMO delivers — provided you're comfortable with a gradual process and don't mind the occasional dusty application on windy days.
products.review:
Unlike liquid poultice cleaners that require pouring, drying, and sweeping away residue, EXIMO is a dry powder you sprinkle directly onto oil stains — no messy wet phase or waiting for evaporation. The natural mineral powder absorbs the oil and gradually breaks it down, and there's no rinsing or scrubbing involved. That keeps the process entirely hands-off after application, a stark contrast to pressure-washing or chemical scrubbing routines that most driveway cleaners demand.

This cleaner fits homeowners who have several oil spots on a driveway or garage floor and want a no-hose approach. It thrives in sheltered areas or on calm days because the fine powder is at the mercy of wind. Deep, set-in stains are a marathon, not a sprint — expect to reapply over time for full removal. If you need a single-application solution, the pour-on liquid alternative in this lineup delivers faster, more aggressive action with a bit of hands-on sweeping afterward.

💡 💡 Tip: Apply on a calm day or shield the area with a cardboard barrier to minimize powder drift during sprinkling.

products.title: Simple Green Oxy Solve Concrete and Driveway Pressure Washer Cleaner, Purple, Unscented, 128 Fl.Oz
products.label: Simple Green Oxy Solve Concrete and Driveway Pressure Washer Cleaner
products.oneLiner: Peroxide-powered pressure washer concentrate that lifts light dirt and mildew, biodegradable.
pros: Biodegradable peroxide formula breaks down mildew and organic stains without harsh solvents.
Pressure washer compatibility ensures even coverage and reduces manual scrubbing.
One-gallon size offers enough product for multiple driveway cleanings.
Clears away light grime and dirt, restoring a fresh look to concrete surfaces.
cons: On driveways with deep-set oil or rust stains, the formula can leave behind visible residue rather than fully lifting the marks. — 51 mentions of stain removal, 55% negative — users note it does not fully remove difficult stains
products.verdict: If your concrete mostly battles dirt and algae, this biodegradable formula keeps it looking clean between occasional deep treatments; for oil-stained pavement, however, you'll need a poultice remover.
products.review:
Simple Green Oxy Solve swaps a gentler all-purpose cleaner (like the brand's multi-surface option) for a peroxide-driven formula built around pressure washer compatibility. It lifts mildew and surface grime from concrete without heavy solvents, making it a practical choice for routine driveway upkeep.

Hook it up to a standard pressure washer, and the solution sprays on evenly, breaking down typical dirt, algae, and mildew without the need for scrubbing. The clean is subtle—it won't erase years of neglect, but it will restore a fresh look to regularly maintained driveways.

Who it's for: homeowners with driveways that see consistent light dirt, mold, and mildew, and who already own a pressure washer. It's less suited for anyone wrestling with deep oil or rust stains—this is a maintenance product, not a degreaser. Buyers on a tight budget may also find more concentrated options for less.

products.title: Simple Green Oxy Solve Total Outdoor Cleaner Concentrate, Pressure Washer & Manual Use, EPA Safer Choice Certified, Peroxide Cleaner for Wood, Concrete, Vinyl & More
products.label: Simple Green Outdoor Cleaner
products.oneLiner: EPA Safer Choice concentrate safe for plants and pets, covers up to 6,400 sq ft.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: For set-in mildew, apply undiluted and scrub with a stiff brush before rinsing to boost stain removal.
pros: Safe for use around plants, pets, and children — peroxide-powered, bleach-free. — 31 mentions, 100% positive
Restores wood, vinyl, concrete, and brick to a like-new appearance. — 36 mentions, 83% positive
Delivers thorough cleaning when applied with a pressure washer. — 187 mentions, 70% positive
Concentrated formula covers up to 6,400 sq ft per bottle.
cons: Struggles on deeply embedded mildew and set-in stains; extra scrubbing may be needed. — 90 mentions of stain removal, 40% negative
Not a degreaser — caked-on oil and grease stains won't lift without a poultice product.
products.verdict: With its EPA Safer Choice certification and plant-safe formula, this concentrate shines for large-scale, routine cleaning. When the stain is more grease than grime, the top pick's poultice system is the clearer path.
products.review:
Simple Green's Oxy Solve Total Outdoor Cleaner sets itself apart as a non-toxic, peroxide-powered concentrate that covers up to 6,400 sq ft per bottle — safe for use around plants, pets, and children. Unlike harsh chemical degreasers or poultice powders that target oil, this formula lifts dirt, pollen, and light mildew from wood, vinyl, concrete, brick, and composite siding without bleach or corrosive solvents.

Paired with a pressure washer, it restores driveways, decks, and fences to a like-new appearance, with impressive results on general grime. The EPA Safer Choice certification backs its gentler profile, making it a practical choice for households where runoff into flower beds or lawns is a concern.

It fits eco-conscious homeowners maintaining large hardscapes who prioritize plant safety over heavy-duty stain removal. On deeply embedded mildew and set-in stains, however, performance tapers off — scrubbing or a dedicated organic-growth remover may be needed. Caked-on oil and grease are beyond its scope; for petroleum stains, the top pick's poultice system remains the more effective path.

💡 💡 Tip: For set-in mildew, apply undiluted and scrub with a stiff brush before rinsing to boost stain removal.

products.title: KÄRCHER Concrete & Driveway Cleaner, Cleans Concrete, Brick, Mansory, and Stone Areas, Highly Concentrated, Makes up to 40 gal of Cleaner, Readily Biodegradable, For Pressure Washers
products.label: Karcher Driveway Cleaner
products.badge: Best Concentrated Formula for Large Jobs
products.oneLiner: Highly concentrated, makes 40 gallons from one gallon for large driveway coverage.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Measure with the cap and aim for a 1:40 dilution; under-mixing turns the solution too weak for effective cleaning.
pros: One gallon produces up to 40 gallons of cleaning solution, lowering cost per application.
Readily biodegradable formula aligns with environmentally conscious use. — spec_highlights.Eco-features: 'Readily biodegradable'
Compatible with pressure washers, foam cannons, and manual scrubbing. — spec_highlights.Application: 'Pressure washer, foam cannon, or manual'
Concentrated liquid saves storage space compared to multiple pre-diluted jugs.
cons: Oil-stain removal may fall short of poultice powders on deep, set-in petroleum marks.
Designed primarily for Kärcher equipment; foam cannon output can vary with other brands.
Achieving the full 40-gallon yield demands exact dilution—getting the ratio wrong can leave grime behind.
products.verdict: For Kärcher owners managing broad driveways, this concentrate makes bulk coverage affordable and eco-friendlier—just plan on a separate oil-stain treatment when petroleum spots are the main concern.
products.review:
One gallon of this biodegradable concentrate creates up to 40 gallons of ready-to-use cleaner—enough to treat a large driveway several times over. The formula is built to work with Kärcher pressure washers and foam cannons, pulling general dirt, mildew, and surface grime off concrete and asphalt without leaving a harsh chemical residue. That scale and compatibility set it apart from peroxide-based mildew cleaners that target organic growth but don't stretch nearly as far per bottle.

Kärcher owners maintaining expansive paved areas will appreciate the bulk coverage. This is best treated as a maintenance detergent for light to moderate soiling, not a degreaser. Set-in oil stains may prove stubborn—poultice products absorb petroleum from the pores of concrete, while a detergent approach often struggles to fully lift deep, old spots. Dilution precision matters too; mixing too weak undercuts cleaning power, so measuring carefully is part of the routine.

💡 💡 Tip: Measure with the cap and aim for a 1:40 dilution; under-mixing turns the solution too weak for effective cleaning.

products.label: 30 SECONDS Outdoor Cleaner
pros: Quick mold, mildew, and algae removal without scrubbing — 1713 mentions, 79% positive
Simple hose-end spray covers large areas fast — 386 mentions, 85% positive
cons: Sprayer head may leak at the handle, wasting product and creating a mess — 211 mentions, 96% negative
Does not remove oil or grease stains
products.verdict: Best for homeowners tackling algae and mold on driveways and siding who don't mind swapping in a dedicated sprayer — but skip it if oil stains are your primary concern.
products.review: 30 SECONDS Outdoor Cleaner handles algae, mold, mildew, and moss on driveways, patios, and siding with a straightforward hose-end application — spray, wait a few minutes, and rinse. There's no scrubbing required, making it convenient for large areas. The formula is safe for plants and lawns when used as directed. However, the product's primary limitation for driveway users is that it cannot remove oil or grease. Petroleum-based stains remain untouched, so you'll need a separate degreaser for those spots. For households where mold and algae are the main complaint, this is a capable biological cleaner, but it's a niche tool rather than an all-in-one driveway solution.

products.title: Wet & Forget Outdoor Moss, Mold, Mildew, & Algae Stain Remover Multi-Surface Cleaner, Ready to Use, 64 Ounce
products.label: Wet & Forget Outdoor Cleaner
pros: Extremely easy application with the included trigger sprayer — just spray and leave. — 227 mentions, 93% positive
No scrubbing or rinsing required; works gradually with rain and sun.
Multi-surface formula safe for use on concrete, asphalt, pavers, and wood.
cons: Very slow acting — green stains may clear in 1-2 weeks, but black and thicker growth can take months and may need repeated applications. — 1496 mentions of effectiveness, 47% negative — many report no improvement
Does not remove oil, grease, or rust stains — strictly for organic growth like moss, mold, mildew, and algae.
products.verdict: Only a fit for homeowners with light algae or mildew who value a hands-off approach and don't mind waiting weeks — and who have no oil stains to address.
products.review:
Wet & Forget Outdoor Cleaner strips away the effort from tackling moss, mold, mildew, and algae on patios, driveways, and siding. The ready-to-use trigger sprayer means you don't mix, scrub, or rinse. A quick spray is all it takes; rain and sunlight gradually break down the organic growth over time, making it a genuinely low-effort maintenance option.

The catch is that it works only on organic growth and only very slowly. Green algae may fade in a week or two, but black stains can take months, and oil, grease, or rust won't budge at all. That confines its usefulness to patient maintenance of light organic buildup, not tackling the oil stains many driveway cleaners target.

products.title: Zep Cleaner Concrete DRVWY 128OZ ZUCON128
products.label: Zep Concrete Driveway Cleaner
pros: Budget-friendly gallon of concentrated cleaner covers many routine washings of light dirt.
Compatible with pressure washers, scrub brushes, or hose-end sprayers for flexible application.
cons: Oil and grease stain removal is inconsistent; set-in petroleum marks may show no visible improvement. — 61 effectiveness mentions, 49% negative — no visible results reported by some users
products.verdict: A sensible choice for routine driveway washing when you don't have set-in oil or grease stains to tackle — just avoid any expectations of degreasing power.
products.review: This budget-friendly gallon of Zep cleaner handles light dirt, dust, and organic grime on concrete and asphalt without heavy scrubbing. It mixes with water to cover a large driveway surface, stretching its value for regular wash-downs. For routine maintenance, it strips away surface-level buildup and restores a clean look, compatible with pressure washers, scrub brushes, or hose-end sprayers. Heavier oil and grease stains fall outside its design envelope; a dedicated degreaser or poultice is the better tool for those jobs. It's a maintenance wash, not a heavy-duty stain remover.

buyingGuide.heading: Intended Stain Type
buyingGuide.body:
Oil and grease stain removers rely on poultice powders or solvent blends that absorb or dissolve hydrocarbons. Poultice formulations draw oil out of concrete pores into a drying powder you sweep away. Organic stain removers for algae, mold, and mildew use oxidizing agents like sodium hypochlorite or peroxide to kill the growth and lift the discoloration.

If you spray a mildew cleaner on motor oil, the oil repels the water-based formula and no cleaning occurs. Pouring an oil poultice over black mold leaves the organisms untouched. For driveways with both stain types, plan on using two separate products in sequence.

buyingGuide.heading: Application Method
buyingGuide.body:
Powder-based waterless cleaners require no hose or pressure washer—you simply sprinkle or pour, let them dry, and sweep. This is perfect for garages or carports without outdoor water access, but the fine dust demands calm conditions. Pressure washer cleaners inject the formula through the machine for high-pressure agitation, which accelerates dirt removal but demands that you already own a pressure washer.

Hose-end sprayers and trigger bottles are convenient for organic cleaners, yet the included sprayers are often the weakest link—leaks and clogs are common. A dedicated pump garden sprayer or foam cannon usually delivers more consistent results.

buyingGuide.heading: Concentration & Coverage
buyingGuide.body:
A one-gallon concentrate that claims to make 40 gallons of ready-to-use cleaner sounds economical, but dilution accuracy is everything. Too weak and it won't clean; too strong and it can leave hazy residues or damage delicate surfaces. Always measure with a dedicated mixing container, not by eye.

For large driveways, prioritize products with stated square-foot coverage so you don't run out mid-job. Small oil-specific bottles cover a handful of stains, while multi-surface concentrates may treat several thousand square feet in one mixing.

buyingGuide.heading: Eco & Plant Safety
buyingGuide.body:
Peroxide-powered and biodegradable cleaners are gentler on surrounding grass and shrubs, earning EPA Safer Choice labels in some cases. They trade raw stain-fighting aggression for environmental safety—bleach-based formulas kill algae faster but can brown nearby lawn edges.

If your driveway is lined with landscaping or your cleaning runoff drains directly into garden beds, a plant-safe option prevents unintended damage. For isolated concrete slabs far from vegetation, more aggressive chemistries pose minimal risk.

faq.question: Can a driveway cleaner remove both oil and mold stains?
faq.answer: No single product does both effectively. Oil removers use poultice or degreaser chemistries, while mold cleaners rely on oxidation to kill organisms. You'll need an oil-specific treatment and a separate mildew cleaner, applied sequentially.

faq.question: Why does my driveway cleaner leave white powder after drying?
faq.answer: Poultice-based oil removers work by absorbing the oil into a powder that you then sweep away. The white residue is the spent poultice carrying the extracted contaminants—brush or vacuum it up, it's a sign the product worked, not a defect.

faq.question: Do I need a pressure washer for driveway cleaner, or can I just use a hose?
faq.answer: It depends on the formula. Many cleaners work with garden sprayers or hose-end attachments; pressure washers add mechanical force for embedded grime. Poultice powders need no water at all—just pour, let dry, and sweep. Pick your method based on your equipment and stain type.

faq.question: How long does it take for a driveway cleaner to work on old oil stains?
faq.answer: Deep-set stains often require multiple applications over days or weeks. Poultice treatments like CHOMP! typically dry within a day and can be reapplied. EXIMO's natural breakdown may take weeks. Patience and repetition are essential for heavily soaked concrete.

faq.question: How do I avoid leaky sprayers when applying driveway cleaner?
faq.answer: Skip the included plastic sprayer and use a dedicated pump garden sprayer or your pressure washer's foam cannon. The cleaners themselves are usually fine, but the disposable applicators are a common frustration point.
